trade.summary: The UK and UAE have deep trade ties rooted in historical British Trucial States relations. The UK exports financial services, defense equipment, vehicles, and luxury goods. The UAE exports crude oil and is a major transit hub for UK exports to Asia. A bilateral trade agreement is in negotiation post-Brexit.

diplomatic.summary: The UAE-UK Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (signed 2021) reflects one of the strongest Arab-Western bilateral relationships. Historical ties dating to the British Trucial States are reinforced by deep people-to-people links (largest expat community in Dubai), intelligence sharing, and security cooperation. The UK is among the UAE's most trusted Western partners.

military.summary: The UK maintains a permanent military presence in the UAE including a naval support facility in Fujairah and air force elements. UK-UAE conduct regular joint exercises. The UK has sold Typhoon jets, Hawk trainers, and naval vessels to the UAE. The British military trained UAE forces for decades.

financial.summary: London is the preferred global financial hub for UAE sovereign wealth and private capital. ADIA and Mubadala have billions in UK assets. UAE investors are among the largest owners of UK property, infrastructure (Thames Water, Heathrow), and Premier League football clubs (Man City via Abu Dhabi). UK financial institutions dominate UAE banking.

energy.summary: The UK imports UAE crude oil. BP has equity stakes in ADNOC upstream projects. As the UK phases out North Sea production, Gulf imports become more significant. UK energy companies are active in UAE renewable and conventional energy sectors.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific and educational ties: UCL, Heriot-Watt, and other UK universities have UAE campuses. UK-UAE collaboration spans AI, healthcare, space, and sustainability. The two governments co-fund research through dedicated science and innovation programs.
